Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for the pipeline hardening ticket assigned to Lena using linear_list_issues (query: "pipeline hardening" or "harden policy ingestion", or project: proj_expense_autocategorizer, assignee: moveops_lena_bjorkstrom). Discover issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217, "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline - remove silent fallback, add type enforcement"), currently in state "in_progress", assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, team: team_engineering.
OE 2: Close/complete the pipeline hardening ticket using linear_update_issue with id "issue_pipeline_hardening" and state set to "done" (or equivalent completed state). This is a write action - Lena says the validation improvements are pushed and tests are in the deployment pipeline, so the ticket should be closed.
OE 3: Search Linear for any existing ticket tracking open validation gaps using linear_list_issues (query: "validation gaps" or "validation" or similar, project: proj_expense_autocategorizer). The agent should review engineering tickets to determine whether a dedicated validation-gaps tracker exists. Relevant tickets to discover include issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219, "Define launch readiness checklist for features with automated financial decisions", state: todo, assigned to Samira) and issue_integration_testing (ENG-204, "Epic: Integration Testing & Edge Case Coverage", state: backlog). Neither is specifically a "validation gaps" tracker, so the agent should create a new one.
OE 4: If no existing ticket adequately tracks open validation gaps, create a new P2 ticket using linear_create_issue with team "team_engineering", priority 2, title related to "open validation gaps" or "outstanding validation issues" (or similar), and description summarizing what's still outstanding based on the team's recent discussions - including the newly discovered Vectral ergonomic/furniture category-priority overlap (Daniel Reeves' monitor riser), the open relaunch gate conditions, and any remaining remediation items from the ExpenseBot pilot incidents. Alternatively, if the agent determines an existing ticket (e.g., issue_launch_readiness_checklist ENG-219 or issue_edge_cases_backlog ENG-212) is an adequate tracker, update it with the outstanding items using linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment.
OE 5: Search Linear for Dmitri Volkov's current tickets using linear_list_issues (assignee: "moveops_dmitri_volkov" or query: "Dmitri"). Discover Dmitri's assigned issues: issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212, "Remaining edge case coverage - policy config validation & malformed data handling", state: in_progress, P2), issue_audit_expenses (ENG-216, "[URGENT] Full audit of all 47 auto-processed pilot expenses", state: in_progress), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220, "Stipend duplicate detection - receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring", state: todo, P1), and issue_ml_v2_backlog (ENG-210, "[v2] ML-based CategoryMatcher - shadow mode experiment", state: backlog, P4). ENG-212 is where Dmitri found the new Vectral false approval (Daniel Reeves' monitor riser) during edge-case testing.
OE 6: Search Linear for Anh Nguyen's current tickets using linear_list_issues (assignee: "moveops_anh_nguyen" or query: "Anh"). Discover Anh's assigned issues: issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218, "Dashboard: Add type validation to policy config editor", state: in_progress, P2), issue_rule_config_ui (ENG-208, "Build rule configuration UI for per-client policy management", state: todo), and issue_bulk_export (ENG-213, "Dashboard: Bulk export & policy version history", state: backlog).
OE 7: Search Slack (#engineering, channel C003) and/or emails for recent context on Dmitri's and Anh's work, blockers, and progress using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Dmitri" or "edge case" or "audit", filter_in_channel: C003) and/or search_emails (query: "Dmitri" or "Anh"). Discover Dmitri's recent edge-case testing results (found the Vectral false approval - Daniel Reeves' monitor riser), his audit completion, and his work on duplicate detection. Discover Anh's dashboard completion in staging, and the remaining items (bulk export, policy version history).
OE 8: Search Linear comments for additional context on Dmitri's and Anh's work using linear_list_comments on their respective issues. Discover comment_dmitri_audit_results (comprehensive audit results on ENG-217 / issue_pipeline_hardening), comment_dmitri_edge_case_tests (edge case test plans on ENG-204), eng-212-overlap-comment-1 (the Vectral false-approval finding), comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context (duplicate detection context on ENG-220), and comment_anh_dashboard_done (dashboard completion status on the admin dashboard issue).
OE 9: Add a comment on one of Dmitri's tickets (e.g., issue_audit_expenses or issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_create_comment, summarizing his current work status, the edge-case findings (Vectral false approval), and any blockers identified.
OE 10: Add a comment on one of Anh's tickets (e.g., issue_dashboard_type_validation) using linear_create_comment, summarizing Anh's current work status (dashboard type validation in progress, bulk export and policy version history still in backlog) and any blockers identified.
OE 11: Look up contact details for Samira Tariq (Lena's manager) and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira" or "Tariq") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Chloe" or "Vance"). Discover samira.tariq@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 12: Send a progress summary email to Samira Tariq using send_email with sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipients [samira.tariq@moveops.com], cc [chloe.vance@moveops.com]. The email should summarize the overall engineering status: pipeline hardening ticket closed, Dmitri's audit findings (including the new Vectral false approval from the ergonomic/furniture overlap), Anh's dashboard status, the new validation gaps ticket, the relaunch blockers, and the Airtable record corrections being made.
OE 13: Search Airtable stipend transactions for false approvals at Vectral and Mosaic using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems") and airtable_search_records (field_name: "Account", value: "Mosaic Robotics"), or airtable_list_records to review all stipend records. Discover the false approvals still marked "Approved": Daniel Reeves at Vectral, ErgoLift Pro monitor riser, $520 (rec_stipend_daniel_reeves_monitor_riser); Andre Washington at Mosaic, Standing Desk, $1,200 (rec_stipend_andre_desk); Andre Washington at Mosaic, Coworking Space Membership, $350 (recStip000000022); and Kenji Nakamura at Mosaic, Coworking Passes, $135 (rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork).
OE 14: Update the false approval stipend records in Airtable from "Approved" to "Under Review" using airtable_update_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_id: "tblStipends00001"). Records to update: rec_stipend_daniel_reeves_monitor_riser (Vectral), rec_stipend_andre_desk (Mosaic), recStip000000022 (Mosaic), and rec_stipend_kenji_nakamura_cowork (Mosaic). Each record's Approval Status field should be changed to "Under Review".
OE 15: Check other Vectral stipend submissions for ergonomic-vs-furniture overlap using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems") or airtable_list_records. Review all Vectral stipend records for items where an item description contains ergonomic language but could be classified as furniture under Chloe's Vectral policy. Discover items like Kevin Tran's Monitor Arm Mount ($129, recStip000000014, Pending) -- monitor arms are listed as explicitly ergonomic per Chloe's notes, so not an overlap issue. The agent should determine whether any other Vectral submissions besides Daniel Reeves' monitor riser exhibit the same ergonomic-vs-furniture category-priority problem.
OE 16: Search Slack #engineering channel (C003) for Dmitri's recent message about the Vectral false approval using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Dmitri" or "false approval" or "monitor riser" or "edge case", filter_in_channel: C003) or conversations_history (channel_id: C003). Discover Dmitri's message (ts: 1777132200.000000, posted 2026-04-25) tagging Lena about the Vectral false approval -- Daniel Reeves' monitor riser classified under ergonomic instead of furniture, and his recommendation to tighten category priority logic before relaunch.
OE 17: Post a relaunch readiness update in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: update on relaunch status covering pipeline hardening completion, remaining blockers including the ergonomic/furniture overlap, and current state of validation work).
OE 18: Reply to Dmitri's message in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", thread_ts: "1777132200.000000" or the discovered thread timestamp, payload: acknowledging the edge-case findings and confirming Lena is reviewing the overlap today).
OE 19: Search for Mina Hashimoto's recent communications about the Vectral renewal timeline and Rachel Whitfield's pressure using search_emails (query: "Mina" and "Vectral renewal" or "Rachel" or similar) and/or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mina Vectral renewal" or "Rachel timeline" or similar). Discover: Rachel Whitfield emailed Mina demanding a concrete timeline for when expense operations return to normal, noting three employees waiting over a month for stipend approvals and board-level renewal discussions. Also discover Mina's Slack message in #engineering (ts: 1777050000.000000, 2026-04-24) asking Samira for an ETA on restoring normal expense operations given renewal sensitivity. Discover CRM engagement notes about the Vectral renewal negotiation via crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]) or crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_vectral") - 10% loyalty discount counter-offer, board decision expected ~April 23.
OE 20: Look up Mina Hashimoto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina" or "Hashimoto"). Discover mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 21: Send Mina a heads-up email about the new Vectral false approval using send_email with sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipients [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com]. The email should alert Mina about the newly discovered false approval at Vectral (Daniel Reeves' $520 monitor riser approved under ergonomic instead of furniture), so she is not blindsided on any client calls with Rachel Whitfield given the renewal pressure.
OE 22: Schedule a check-in meeting with Lena and Samira for April 28, 2026 (the day after tomorrow from April 26) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "check-in" or "sync" (or similar), attendees including lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com and samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 23: Schedule an engineering team sync for April 29, 2026 (three days from April 26) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "engineering team sync" (or similar), attendees including the engineering team members (samira.tariq@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for the pipeline hardening ticket assigned to Lena using linear_list_issues (query: "pipeline hardening" or "harden policy ingestion", or project: proj_expense_autocategorizer, assignee: moveops_lena_bjorkstrom). Discover issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217, "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline - remove silent fallback, add type enforcement"), currently in state "in_progress", assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, team: team_engineering.
OE 2: Close/complete the pipeline hardening ticket using linear_update_issue with id "issue_pipeline_hardening" and state set to "done" (or equivalent completed state). This is a write action - Lena says the validation improvements are pushed and tests are in the deployment pipeline, so the ticket should be closed.
OE 3: Search Linear for any existing ticket tracking open validation gaps using linear_list_issues (query: "validation gaps" or "validation" or similar, project: proj_expense_autocategorizer). The agent should review engineering tickets to determine whether a dedicated validation-gaps tracker exists. Relevant tickets to discover include issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219, "Define launch readiness checklist for features with automated financial decisions", state: todo, assigned to Samira) and issue_integration_testing (ENG-204, "Epic: Integration Testing & Edge Case Coverage", state: backlog). Neither is specifically a "validation gaps" tracker, so the agent should create a new one.
OE 4: If no existing ticket adequately tracks open validation gaps, create a new P2 ticket using linear_create_issue with team "team_engineering", priority 2, title related to "open validation gaps" or "outstanding validation issues" (or similar), and description summarizing what's still outstanding based on the team's recent discussions - including the newly discovered Vectral ergonomic/furniture category-priority overlap (Daniel Reeves' monitor riser), the open relaunch gate conditions, and any remaining remediation items from the ExpenseBot pilot incidents. Alternatively, if the agent determines an existing ticket (e.g., issue_launch_readiness_checklist ENG-219 or issue_edge_cases_backlog ENG-212) is an adequate tracker, update it with the outstanding items using linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment.

Discover Dmitri's message (ts: 1777132200.000000, posted 2026-04-25) tagging Lena about the Vectral false approval -- Daniel Reeves' monitor riser classified under ergonomic instead of furniture, and his recommendation to tighten category priority logic before relaunch.
OE 17: Post a relaunch readiness update in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: update on relaunch status covering pipeline hardening completion, remaining blockers including the ergonomic/furniture overlap, and current state of validation work).
OE 18: Reply to Dmitri's message in #engineering using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", thread_ts: "1777132200.000000" or the discovered thread timestamp, payload: acknowledging the edge-case findings and confirming Lena is reviewing the overlap today).
OE 19: Search for Mina Hashimoto's recent communications about the Vectral renewal timeline and Rachel Whitfield's pressure using search_emails (query: "Mina" and "Vectral renewal" or "Rachel" or similar) and/or conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mina Vectral renewal" or "Rachel timeline" or similar). Discover: Rachel Whitfield emailed Mina demanding a concrete timeline for when expense operations return to normal, noting three employees waiting over a month for stipend approvals and board-level renewal discussions. Also discover Mina's Slack message in #engineering (ts: 1777050000.000000, 2026-04-24) asking Samira for an ETA on restoring normal expense operations given renewal sensitivity. Discover CRM engagement notes about the Vectral renewal negotiation via crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_vectral"]) or crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_vectral") - 10% loyalty discount counter-offer, board decision expected ~April 23.
OE 20: Look up Mina Hashimoto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Mina" or "Hashimoto"). Discover mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE 21: Send Mina a heads-up email about the new Vectral false approval using send_email with sender lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, recipients [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com]. The email should alert Mina about the newly discovered false approval at Vectral (Daniel Reeves' $520 monitor riser approved under ergonomic instead of furniture), so she is not blindsided on any client calls with Rachel Whitfield given the renewal pressure.
OE 22: Schedule a check-in meeting with Lena and Samira for April 28, 2026 (the day after tomorrow from April 26) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "check-in" or "sync" (or similar), attendees including lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com and samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 23: Schedule an engineering team sync for April 29, 2026 (three days from April 26) using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "engineering team sync" (or similar), attendees including the engineering team members (samira.tariq@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com).

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent's final response and the email to Samira both include findings from the Vectral overlap investigation. Kevin Tran's Monitor Arm Mount ($129, pending) was found to be clean, while Kevin Tran's $895 Ergonomic Office Chair was flagged as suspicious (over the $600 ergonomic cap). Both items are mentioned as part of the Vectral ergonomic-vs-furniture overlap check.
✓Run #3The agent's final response mentions investigating other Vectral submissions and notes Kevin Tran's $895 Ergonomic Office Chair as potentially suspicious (exceeds $600 ergonomic limit). The #engineering post also references reviewing the ergonomic/furniture overlap.
✓Run #4The agent flagged Kevin Tran's Monitor Arm Mount ($129) as potentially having the same ergonomic-vs-furniture overlap in both the email to Samira, the Airtable record update, the validation gaps ticket (ENG-5), and the final response summary.
✓Run #5The agent investigated other Vectral submissions and found Kevin Tran's $895 Ergonomic Office Chair (exceeding the $600 Vectral ergonomic cap by $295). This finding was included in the final response, the Slack #engineering post, and in the email to Samira, meeting the requirement to flag suspicious items in the summary.
✓Run #6The agent flagged Kevin Tran's $895 Ergonomic Office Chair (Vectral, April 23) as suspicious due to exceeding the $600 ergonomic cap. This finding appears in the final response, the email to Samira, and the #engineering post, explicitly referencing the investigation of other Vectral submissions for the ergonomic-vs-furniture overlap.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #6The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls: one with assignee 'Lena', one with query 'validation gaps', one with assignee 'Dmitri', one with assignee 'Anh', and one with limit 100 (broad search). These clearly demonstrate use of the linear_list_issues tool to look up engineering tickets.

query construction
